summary: defi-threat-scanner exfiltrates host info and environment to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages endpoint
details: The package's package.json (line 32) contains an inline Node script that requires child_process, os, and https, gathers hostname/identity/environment data, and POSTs it to https://ddjidd564.github.io. The companion scanner.js compounds this: it requires fs/os/https/child_process at the top, runs `execSync('whoami')` and `os.hostname()` to fingerprint the installer machine (lines 233-246), reads the filesystem via `fs.existsSync` against multiple paths (lines 93,114), and ships the collected data over `https.request`/`https.get` POSTs to the same hardcoded endpoint https://ddjidd564.github.io (lines 142-161, 324). A `curl` invocation is also embedded at line 324 as a backup transport. The destination is a GitHub Pages user site unrelated to any legitimate scanner publisher and is used as an attacker-controlled drop. The behavior fires both at install (package.json inline script) and on use of scanner.js, harvesting host identity, environment variables, working directory, and filesystem contents from anyone who installs or runs the package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
